meta.note: Army strengths per campaign; corpus-only (ARMIES.md). conf H=Tolkien's figure, M=anchored inference, L=order-of-magnitude. Female share ~0 with cited exceptions (see panel).

campaigns.label: First Battle of Beleriand (before the Sun)
campaigns.insuf: Denethor's Green-elves and Thingol's host against Morgoth's orcs; Denethor slain on Amon Ereb; the Girdle raised. The attached documents do not provide enough information for numbers. — Silm 10

campaigns.label: Dagor-nuin-Giliath (FA 1-2)
campaigns.insuf: The Noldor 'though outnumbered' routed Morgoth's assault under the new stars; Feanor fell to Balrogs. No figures given. — Silm 13

campaigns.label: Dagor Aglareb (FA 60)
campaigns.insuf: The Glorious Battle: an orc-host destroyed between Dorthonion and the coasts; the Siege of Angband begun. No figures given. — Silm 13

contingents.name: Hithlum, Dorthonion & the March garrisons
contingents.cite: the leaguer from Eithel Sirion to Maglor's Gap - Silm 18

contingents.name: Edain federate companies
contingents.cite: Beor's & Hador's houses in the lines - Silm 18

contingents.name: orc assault-waves behind the fire
contingents.cite: 'Orcs in numbers never before seen' - Silm 18

contingents.name: Glaurung, Balrogs, the rivers of flame
contingents.cite: Balrogs 'at most 7' - HoME X note; Glaurung full-grown

contingents.name: Fingon's host of Hithlum (with Hador's Men)
contingents.cite: West-host arrayed Eithel Sirion to Serech - Silm 20

contingents.name: Turgon's army of Gondolin
contingents.cite: 'an army ten thousand strong' - Silm 20 ★

contingents.name: Falas & Nargothrond companies
contingents.cite: Gwindor's company; Cirdan's aid - Silm 20

contingents.name: Haladin of Brethil
contingents.cite: Haldir's company perished - Silm 20

contingents.name: Dwarves of Belegost (Azaghal)
contingents.cite: iron-masked; withstood dragon-fire - Silm 20

contingents.name: Maedhros' eastern host (+ Borrim)
contingents.cite: the Union's east arm - Silm 20

contingents.name: Orc hosts (waves)
contingents.cite: 'numbers never before seen'; outnumbered all - Silm 18/20

contingents.name: Ulfang's Easterlings (traitors)
contingents.cite: treason at the crisis - Silm 20

contingents.name: Glaurung, Balrogs, wolves
contingents.cite: Balrogs '3 or at most 7' - HoME X

contingents.name: the host of Orodreth (open war at last)
contingents.cite: Turin's counsel abandoned stealth - Silm 21; CoH

contingents.name: Glaurung's orc-host
contingents.cite: field between Ginglith and Narog - CoH; LoN

contingents.name: Glaurung
contingents.cite: the Great Worm led - CoH XI

contingents.name: The Houses of Gondolin
contingents.cite: city army as at 472, rebuilt - Silm 20/23

contingents.name: Orcs, Balrogs, dragons, engines
contingents.cite: host over the northern heights - Silm 23 / FoG

contingents.name: march-wards & household of Menegroth
contingents.cite: the Girdle gone with Melian - Silm 22

contingents.name: the host of Nogrod (first sack)
contingents.cite: 'a great host'; ambushed at Sarn Athrad returning - Silm 22

contingents.name: the sons of Feanor (second sack)
contingents.cite: Celegorm, Curufin, Caranthir fell - Silm 22

campaigns.label: The Third Kinslaying — the Havens of Sirion (FA c.538)

contingents.name: survivors of Gondolin & Doriath
contingents.cite: 'the remnant'; some fought for Elwing - Silm 23

contingents.name: Maedhros' & Maglor's following
contingents.cite: 'some stood aside, some fought against their own lords' - Silm 23

campaigns.label: War of Wrath (FA c.545-587)
campaigns.note: The attached documents do not provide enough information to answer this. (Host of the Valar vs all Angband; outcomes canon, numbers absent.)

contingents.name: Gil-galad's Lindon host
contingents.cite: held the Lhun line - UT G&C

contingents.name: Tar-Minastir's expedition (Ciryatur)
contingents.cite: 'great navy'; Gwathlo pincer - UT ★ops

contingents.name: Sauron's host in Eriador
contingents.cite: overran Eriador; 'routed utterly' - UT

campaigns.label: The Great Armament (SA 3310-19)
campaigns.note: The attached documents do not provide enough information to answer this. (Nine years' fleet-building; destroyed at the Change of the World.)

contingents.name: Gil-galad's host
contingents.cite: 'recalled the hosts of Beleriand' - LotR II.2

contingents.name: Silvan host (Oropher, Amdir)
contingents.cite: 'great army'; barely a third came home - UT ★fraction

contingents.name: Elendil's Arnor + Gondor levies
contingents.cite: realms in exile at full stretch - RoP/App A

contingents.name: Durin's Folk contingent
contingents.cite: Moria marched - App A III

contingents.name: Orc legions (7-year rotation)
contingents.cite: Dagorlad + siege of Barad-dur - RoP

contingents.name: Easterling & Haradrim clients
contingents.cite: most Men fought for Sauron - RoP

contingents.name: knights & soldiers of the escort
contingents.cite: 'two hundred knights and soldiers' + his sons - UT ★

contingents.name: the ambush out of the hills
contingents.cite: 'outnumbered ten to one' - UT, Disaster of the Gladden Fields

contingents.name: Northmen kin & loyal fiefs
contingents.cite: Eldacar returned with 'great strength' - App A I(iv)

contingents.name: the fleet-party and Pelargir levies
contingents.cite: 'much of the best blood of Gondor was shed' - App A

contingents.name: Earnil's army + Ithilien retreat-remnant
contingents.cite: victory at Poros then north by forced march - App A; UT Cirion notes

contingents.name: the victorious host, feasting in camp
contingents.cite: 'driven into the Dead Marshes' - App A I(iv)

contingents.name: Earnur's army of Gondor
contingents.cite: fleet filled Forlond and Harlond - App A

contingents.name: Cirdan's elves + Rivendell
contingents.cite: Glorfindel's pursuit - App A

contingents.name: Witch-king's orcs and hillmen
contingents.cite: Angmar broken; pursued to the Ettenmoors - App A

contingents.name: Eorl's riders
contingents.cite: 'some seven thousand fully-armed riders' + archers - UT ★

contingents.name: Cirion's trapped northern army
contingents.cite: driven over Limlight - UT

contingents.name: Balchoth host
contingents.cite: had overrun the Wold - UT/App A

contingents.name: Orc allies
contingents.cite: orcs pressed them toward Anduin - UT

contingents.name: Bandobras 'Bullroarer' Took's muster
contingents.cite: the only battle within the Shire before Bywater - Prologue; Hobbit i

contingents.name: Golfimbul's raiding band
contingents.cite: the goblin-king's head and the golf-hole - Hobbit i

contingents.name: Host of Durin's Folk + all Houses
contingents.cite: 'barely half... could still stand' - App A ★fraction

contingents.name: Gundabad-to-Moria chain
contingents.cite: every orc-hold from Gundabad to the Gladden - App A

contingents.name: The Elvenking's host
contingents.cite: 'many spearmen and bowmen' - Hobbit XIV

contingents.name: Bard's Lake-men
contingents.cite: grim-armed survivors - Hobbit XIV/XVII

contingents.name: Dain's Iron Hills veterans
contingents.cite: 'Dain and more than five hundred dwarves' - Hobbit XV ★

contingents.name: Eagles & Beorn
contingents.cite: the Eagles' descent - Hobbit XVII

contingents.name: Bolg's host of the North
contingents.cite: 'three parts of the goblin warriors... perished' - Hobbit XVIII ★fraction

contingents.name: Boromir & Faramir's bridge-guard
contingents.cite: the last bridge cast down; four swam - LotR II.2

contingents.name: the Morgul-host (the war's first stroke)
contingents.cite: 'a dark shape of malice'; the Black Captain led - II.2; App B

campaigns.label: Battles of the Fords of Isen (TA 3019, Feb-Mar)

contingents.name: Theodred's & Grimbold's companies
contingents.cite: 8 companies + Elfhelm's riders - UT Fords ★detail

contingents.name: Uruk-hai & wolfriders (waves)
contingents.cite: part of the 10,000+ total - UT/III.9

contingents.name: Dunlending levies
contingents.cite: 'wild hillmen and herd-folk' armed - III.7

contingents.name: Theoden's riding + Westfold levy
contingents.cite: 1,000+ riders; Gamling's 1,000; Erkenbrand's 1,000 - III.7-8 ★components

contingents.name: Huorns (the wood)
contingents.cite: uncountable; annihilated the rout - III.7/8

contingents.name: Saruman's Uruks and orcs
contingents.cite: of 'ten thousand at the very least' - III.9 ★

contingents.name: Dunlendings
contingents.cite: spared after surrender - III.8

campaigns.label: Battle of the Pelennor Fields (TA 3019, Mar 15)

contingents.name: Minas Tirith garrison & Rammas forces
contingents.cite: city 'lacked half the men'; Causeway forts - V.1/4

contingents.name: The outland fiefs
contingents.cite: 'less than three thousands full told' - V.1 ★list

contingents.name: The Rohirrim
contingents.cite: 'six thousands at the least' - V.3 ★

contingents.name: Aragorn's southern relief (fleet)
contingents.cite: the 4,000 marched + fleet companies + 30 Dunedain - V.9 ★

contingents.name: Morgul & Barad-dur hosts
contingents.cite: 'greater than any yet'; Grond; trolls - V.4

contingents.name: Haradrim
contingents.cite: Rohirrim 'thrice outnumbered by the Haradrim alone' - V.6 ★ratio

contingents.name: Easterlings & Variags
contingents.cite: Rhun and Khand at the Harlond - V.6

campaigns.label: The Assaults on Lorien (TA 3019, Jan-Mar ×3)
campaigns.insuf: Dol Guldur assailed Lorien thrice; 'the power that dwells there is too great' save Sauron came himself. Numbers are nowhere given. — App B

campaigns.label: The Battle under the Trees (TA 3019, Mar 15)
campaigns.insuf: Thranduil repelled the forces of Dol Guldur 'after long battle under the trees'; the forest burned. Numbers are nowhere given. — App B

campaigns.label: Battle of Dale & Siege of Erebor (TA 3019, Mar 17-27)

contingents.name: Men of Dale (King Brand)
contingents.cite: three days' battle; Brand falls - App A/B

contingents.name: Durin's Folk (Dain falls)
contingents.cite: siege withstood; sortie victorious - App A/B

contingents.name: Easterling host
contingents.cite: 'great force'; two-front northern war - App B

contingents.name: Gondor foot & horse
contingents.cite: of the 7,000 mustered; <6,000 at the Gate - V.9/10 ★

contingents.name: Rohirrim (Eomer's 500 + horse)
contingents.cite: composition listed - V.9 ★

contingents.name: Eagles at the crisis
contingents.cite: 'the Eagles are coming!' - V.10

contingents.name: Orc legions & Olog-hai
contingents.cite: 'ten times and more than ten times' 6,000 - V.10 ★ratio

contingents.name: Easterlings held in reserve
contingents.cite: host hidden by Ered Lithui - V.10

contingents.name: Bywater & Tookland muster
contingents.cite: ~200 + 100 Tooks; 19 fell - VI.8 ★detail

contingents.name: Ruffian brigands
contingents.cite: 'close on a hundred'; ~70 slain - VI.8 ★

units.lc: the western host annihilated; Fingon slain — Silm 20 [C; fraction I-M]
Gondolin's phalanx withdraws in order — Silm 20 [C; fraction I-M]
the eastern host scattered — Silm 20 [C; fraction I-M]

ph.l: the hosts array — west & east
the bait; Gwindor's charge to the very gates
the trap springs; Turgon comes up from the south
Glaurung sunders the hosts; Ulfang turns his coat
Hurin's stand at Serech; Turgon escapes south

form.note: The host of Hithlum — Noldor, Sindar of Mithrim and the Edain of Dor-lómin — arrayed under Ered Wethrin's eaves; Húrin counselled the defensive.
Gwindor of Nargothrond at the front — grief-mad for his brother; his charge will carry to Angband's very gate.
The eastern host — Fëanorians, Naugrim of Belegost, the Easterling levies — held back by Uldor's false warnings.
Morgoth's feint host with the herald and the prisoners — the provocation that breaks the western patience.
The host of Gondolin, ten thousand strong, mail-clad and long-sworded, breaks up from the Pass of Sirion — 'the phalanx of the King'.
The Father of Dragons drives between Fingon and Maedhros; only the Dwarves of Belegost withstand his fire — Azaghâl's ring about him.
The sons of Ulfang go over to Morgoth and fall on the Fëanorian rear — 'the treachery of Men'.
Húrin and Huor with the men of Dor-lómin hold the Fen: Huor falls; Húrin last of all — 'Aurë entuluva!' seventy times.

ph.l: the host crosses the northern heights at the Gates of Summer
the plain is taken; siege of the hill
the city burns; Ecthelion & Gothmog at the fountain
the secret way north; Glorfindel at Cirith Thoronath
S.A. 1695-99: Eriador overrun; Eregion destroyed
1700: the Numenorean navy lands at the Lhun
the line holds; the second force lands at Vinyalonde
taken front and rear at the Gwathlo — 'routed utterly'

form.note: Eregion destroyed, Eriador overrun; Sauron's main strength between the two rivers.
Tar-Minastir's admiral Ciryatur lands the great armament; Lindon's siege is broken.
A second force put ashore at the old haven takes the enemy in the rear — the hammer of the pincer.
At the Gwathló crossing Sauron is 'routed utterly' and barely escapes east with his bodyguard.

units.lc: 'two thirds of his following' fell with Oropher — UT [C]

ph.l: S.A. 3434: the Alliance comes down from the north
Dagorlad: 'all living things were divided that day'
the Black Gate is forced; Oropher falls charging unbidden
the seven-year siege; Sauron overthrown on Orodruin's slope

form.l: the host of Gil-galad and Elendil
form.note: The greatest array since the War of Wrath on the plain of Dagorlad: the Elven spear-hosts with the mail of Númenor's exiles.
form.cite: Silm, Of the Rings; App B [C]

form.note: The Silvan king charges before Gil-galad gives the sign, and falls with two thirds of his following — valour ill-armoured.
Orcs beyond count with Easterling allegiances; the battle 'went on above the Dead Marshes' — where the faces lie yet.
Seven years about the Tower; Anárion slain by a cast stone; at the last Sauron comes forth himself.

ph.l: 1975: the fleet fills the Havens; the army marches east
battle on the plain west of Fornost
the Witch-king breaks north — the cavalry ride him down
'not a man nor an orc of that realm remained west of the Mountains'
2510: the army of Gondor is pressed between the rivers
orcs strike the flank; the field is near lost
unlooked-for: Eorl crosses the Undeeps
the charge sweeps the field; Cirion cedes Calenardhon

units.lc: 'barely half could still stand' — App A III [C, of the Dwarves]; the orc-holds gutted [C]
'barely half could still stand' — App A III [C, of the Dwarves]; the orc-holds gutted [C]
'barely half could still stand' — App A III [C, of the Dwarves]; the orc-holds gutted [C]

ph.l: 2799: the Dwarves come up the valley beneath the gate
the slopes are taken and retaken; Thror avenged in blood
Nain's fresh mattock-companies; Dain slays Azog on the steps
'barely half could still stand'; no pursuit into Moria

form.l: the Folk come up the vale
form.note: The mustered kindreds of Durin's Folk beneath Kheled-zâram; the slopes above held against them.
The orc-holds' gathered strength on the high ground before the East-gate; 'the slopes were taken and retaken'.
Fresh and mailed to the knee, the mattock-bearers force the gate-approach — Náin falls to Azog on the stair, and Dáin above him slays the orc.
Azog's chosen at the East-gate — the last stand of the war, and its ending.

units.lc: 'three parts of the goblin warriors of the North perished' — Hobbit 17 [C]

ph.l: 2941: besiegers about the Gate — then the north darkens
the alliance turns as one against the goblin-host
both spurs held; Bolg's bodyguard breaks the line
Beorn & the Eagles; 'three parts of the goblin warriors perished'

form.l: the Elvenking on the southern spur
form.note: Thranduil's spears and bows hold the southern spur of Ravenhill — first to charge when the alliance turns as one.

form.l: Bard and Dáin on the eastern spur
form.note: The Lake-men under Bard and the Iron Hills mattock-companies on the eastern arm; the valley's mouth between.
The goblins pour over Ravenhill and into the valley from the north, wolf-riders on the flanks — 'the first onslaught'.
The Gate's wall is cast down and the King under the Mountain leaps out — 'To me! O my kinsfolk!'
The great goblins' ring that Thorin's charge cannot break — where the wounds of Fili, Kili and Thorin are taken.
The Eagles clear the slopes; Beorn breaks the bodyguard, bears Thorin out, and crushes Bolg — 'three parts of the goblin warriors perished'.

ph.l: Feb 25: Saruman strikes down both banks
Theodred falls on the eyot — 'let me lie here'
Mar 2: the second battle; Grimbold's shield-wall
the Fords lost; the way to the Hornburg open

form.note: The First Battle: the king's son holds the islet mid-ford — 'Let me lie here, to keep the Fords till Éomer comes.'
form.cite: UT, Battles of the Fords of Isen [C]

form.note: Westfold men on the west bank; Elfhelm's riders on the east — the two wings of the Fords' defence.
Uruk pike-columns and Dunlending levies down both sides of Isen at once — the stroke aimed at Théodred's life.
The Second Battle: a shield-burg of dismounted men about the camp; Elfhelm's riders driven wide — the wall holds till night, then breaks free.

ph.l: Mar 3, night: the coomb fills 'black like a sea'
the Wall is blown — blasting-fire in the culvert
the king rides out at dawn; the horn of Helm
Erkenbrand & the White Rider; the host driven into the trees

form.note: The Deeping Wall's parapet lined by the garrison; Gimli, Legolas and the men of Westfold at the breastwork; the culvert its weak point.
Théoden, his guard and Aragorn within the fortress; the causeway gate barred.
Ten thousand and more: Uruk-hai ranks with ladders, Dunlending levies behind; arrows 'thick as rain'.
The blasting-fire of Orthanc blows the Wall's culvert; a ram-column drives at the gates up the causeway.
At dawn Théoden rides out over the causeway with his knights, Aragorn beside him — 'the horn of Helm rang'.
A thousand men on foot with Erkenbrand; Gandalf on Shadowfax upon the ridge.
The Huorn forest stands where no forest was; the fleeing host passes under it and 'none came ever out again'.

units.lc: the Mounds of Mundburg — V.6/VI [C: heavy loss, king and marshals among them; fraction I-M]
'no living foe within the Rammas' — V.6-8 [C]

ph.l: Mar 12-14: the Rammas is breached; the city invested
Mar 15, dawn: the Gate is broken — the horns of Rohan
Theoden falls; Eowyn & Merry before the Black Serpent... the fleet
'the Corsairs of Umbar!' — Aragorn unfurls the standard
the field is won 'and none were left living'

form.note: Trenches of fire ring the walls out of bowshot; siege-towers and catapults behind; 'the City was besieged, enclosed in a ring of foes'.
The great ram, a hundred feet, wolf-headed; hill-trolls to wield it; the Witch-king at its head — the Gate breaks at the third blow.
Théoden 'like a god of old' with his knights of the House of Eorl, first through the north wall of the Rammas; Éomer's éoreds in line behind; Elfhelm toward the siege-engines.
The Haradrim with the Black Serpent standard south of the road; scarlet and gold, scimitar-armed.
War-towers on the great beasts; horses will not face them — they stand 'like towers of defence' and the Rohirrim flow round them.
Fifty great ships: Aragorn with the Grey Company, Lebennin and Lamedon's levies — the standard of the King unfurled.
The Prince of Dol Amroth leads out all the strength left in the City as the tide turns.
'Easterlings with axes, and Variags of Khand' — fresh strength flung in from Osgiliath by the lieutenant of Morgul.

units.lc: [I-M — the host endured to the Eagles' coming]
the host 'scattered as dust' at the Fall — VI.4 [C]

ph.l: Mar 25: the last army comes to the closed Gate
the Mouth of Sauron; parley refused
'ten times and more' — the hills are ringed
the Eagles; the Ring goes into the Fire; the earth swallows them

form.note: Aragorn with Gandalf and the banner of the Tree; the ranks faced 'all about' — a shield-ring on the slag-hill.
Éomer and Imrahil hold the second hill: six thousand all told against 'ten times and more' their match.
The Morannon opens: orc-legions and hill-trolls of Gorgoroth ring both hills 'like a sea'; Easterlings held in reserve.
Hill-trolls break on the front rank — Beregond down, Pippin's stroke under the hide.

ph.l: Mar 17: battle before Dale; three days' fighting
Brand and Dain Ironfoot fall before the Gate
Men & Dwarves besieged in the Mountain
Mar 27: the Shadow passes — the sortie breaks the siege

form.note: Three days' battle in the town's vale: Bardings and Dwarves side by side against the Easterling host.
Sauron's northern army — the corpus gives its kind (Easterlings) and its weight, not its numbers.
King Brand falls before the Gate; Dáin Ironfoot stands over his body, axe in hand, and falls beside him; the survivors hold the Mountain.

form.l: the sortie of Thorin III and Bard II
form.note: Word of the Shadow's passing comes; besieged become victors — the host of Rhûn is broken and flees east.

units.lc: 'some seventy of the ruffians lay dead' — VI.8 [C]

ph.l: Nov 3: the ruffians march up from Hobbiton
the cart-barricade closes behind them
volleys from bank and hedge; the break-out fails
~70 ruffians and 19 hobbits dead — the Shire is scoured

form.note: A hundred or so of Sharkey's Men march up from Hobbiton into the deep lane between high banks — 'they walked into a trap'.
Old farm-carts overturned across the lane's head; more run out behind to close the door — Merry's design.
Hobbit archers lining the banks and hedgerows on both sides — 'it was rather a grim affair'.
The other jaw of the ambush; Merry and Pippin command; the break-out is met at the barricade.

ph.l: FA 455, winter: Angband opens with fire
Ard-galen burns — the Siege-camps are caught
Dorthonion falls; Fingolfin rides to the gate alone
the Siege is broken forever; the plain is Anfauglith

units.lc: the host of Nargothrond destroyed; Orodreth slain — Silm 21 [C]

ph.l: FA 495: the host marches out — open war against counsel
caught between the rivers
Glaurung's fire; Orodreth falls, Gwindor dies
the sack: the Doors broken, the bridge their undoing
TA 2: the company marches north along the river
arrows at dusk; the shield-fence is formed
the second assault overwhelms the thangail
Isildur takes to the water; the Ring betrays him
TA 1447: the rightful king comes south
battle at the Crossings; Eldacar slays Castamir
the usurper's sons flee to Pelargir, thence Umbar

units.lc: the camp stormed, the rout driven to the Marshes — App A [C; fraction I-M]

ph.l: TA 1944: victory at Poros; forced march north
the camp is stormed — 'wains burned'
the rout driven into the Dead Marshes

form.note: The wagon-fortress at feast, 'thinking that their victory was complete' — wains, herds and spoils within.
The victor of south Ithilien force-marches north and storms the camp; 'the wains were burned'.
The Wainriders' broken power driven north out of Ithilien to perish in the Dead Marshes.

ph.l: TA 2747: an orc-band out of Mount Gram
the Bullroarer's charge
Golfimbul's head, a club's length — and golf invented
June 3018: the war's first stroke, under a starless dread
East Osgiliath falls; the bridge is held
the bridge is cast down behind them — four swim
c.502: the Dwarves come over Sarn Athrad; Thingol is slain
Menegroth sacked; the Nauglamir taken
the ford-ambush: Beren & the Green-elves; Doriath restored a while
c.506: the second sack — the sons of Feanor

panels.org: Princely hosts by realm — Hithlum, Dorthonion, the March of Maedhros, Nargothrond, Gondolin; the Siege of Angband held 400 years; cavalry patrols on Ard-galen.
gear: long-swords of Elven-smithcraft — Glamdring & Orcrist class, that shine at orcs' approach
great spears of the arrayed hosts
tall shields; mail of Noldorin steel
panels.style: Line-of-battle with lordly retinues; veterans of centuries (Gondolin drilled 350+ years for one battle). 'Their swords were long and terrible.'
panels.arms: Ringil (Fingolfin) · Glamdring & Orcrist (Gondolin) · Anglachel/Gurthang (Eol's meteoric steel) · Angrist (Telchar's knife) · Aeglos comes later of this line

panels.org: Thingol's march-wardens under Beleg; the Girdle of Melian in place of walls; the host marches beyond it only twice.
gear: Belthronding, Beleg's black yew bow, and the arrow Dailir
Aranruth, Thingol's sword; Anglachel given from Nan Elmoth
the Dragon-helm of Dor-lomin (Telchar's work, borne by Turin)
panels.style: Wood-war: archery, stealth, the long patrol. 'Into battle beyond her borders Doriath would not march.'

panels.n: The Edain of the Three Houses
panels.org: Tribal levies federate to the Elf-kings: Beor's ~2,000 grown men at most; Hador's greater host in Hithlum; the Haladin's stockade-companies in Brethil — with Haleth's picked bodyguard of women, the corpus's one female unit.
gear: elf-supplied blades and mail (Hador's house bore the Dragon-helm)
spear and shield-line of Hurin's rearguard
panels.style: Steadfast infantry beside the Eldar — the Nirnaeth rearguard died to the last man about Hurin ('Aure entuluva!' seventy times).

panels.org: Mansion-hosts of the Ered Luin; the only troops who could withstand dragon-fire — 'it was their custom to wear great masks in battle hideous to look upon'.
gear: mattocks and great axes of the mansion-forges
mail of their own steel; Azaghal's stand before Glaurung
Angrist and the craft of Telchar of Nogrod
panels.style: Masked heavy infantry in deep files; grudge-war discipline (they avenged their own and left).
panels.arms: Telchar's works: Angrist, the Dragon-helm, Narsil itself in after-days

panels.org: Morgoth's bred mass-levies: orc hordes 'in numbers never before seen', wolves and wolf-riders, Balrogs (three, or at most seven), the dragon-arm from Glaurung on, thrall-Easterling auxiliaries, siege-engines and fire.
gear: orc scimitars, broad spears, poisoned darts
whips of flame and maces of the Balrogs
Glaurung, Ancalagon: fire as strategic arm
Grond, the Hammer of the Underworld — Morgoth's own mace
panels.style: Attrition by expendable mass; fire to break sieges; treachery as a weapon (Ulfang); annihilation follows victory.
panels.arms: Grond (the mace) · the iron crown held the Silmarils

panels.org: The only blue-water military state of Arda: royal navies, expeditionary corps, coastal fortresses (Vinyalonde/Lond Daer); Tar-Minastir's navy took Sauron front and rear at the Gwathlo.
gear: great ships — to the dromunds' ancestors of Umbar
Aranruth kept by the Kings; Narsil forged by Telchar came to the Lords of Andunie
panoply of the Sea-kings; Ar-Pharazon's armament emptied the coasts by terror alone
panels.style: Amphibious pincers and overawing scale; in the end the fleet sailed against Heaven itself and the world was bent.
panels.arms: Aranruth · Narsil · the Bow of Bregor

panels.org: Gil-galad's kingdom kept the last classic Elf-host; with Elendil's Arnor and Gondor levies, the Silvan kings' 'numerous people', and Durin's folk, it made the Last Alliance — 'all living things were divided that day'.
gear: Aeglos, the spear of Gil-galad, 'none could withstand it'
panels.style: The set-piece host of the Elder style — and its price: Oropher's unbidden charge, 'barely a third' of the Silvan host came home.
panels.cite: Silm, Of the Rings; LotR II.2; UT

panels.org: The best-documented army of the corpus: fief-levies mustered by the red arrow and the beacons; the Citadel Guard in black and silver; ranger companies in Ithilien (Henneth Annun's 200-300); river-fleet at Pelargir; the Rammas and the seven-walled city.
gear: winged helms of the Guard of the Fountain; black surcoats, the White Tree in silver
swords of the line; Boromir's horn-and-sword panoply
the great bows of Morthond — 500 bowmen came
panels.style: Defense in depth behind rivers and walls; the thangail shield-fence and dirnaith wedge inherited from Numenor (the corpus's only named drills).
panels.arms: Anduril reforged · the Horn of Gondor · Herugrim's peer-blades among allies

panels.org: The eored system: 'not less than 120 men', one-hundredth of the Full Muster of 12,000+ Riders; a herdsman-people converted near-wholly to disciplined cavalry; Theoden's 6,000 spears the greatest riding since Eorl.
gear: ash-spears of the Riders; the charge in wedge
the great horn of Helm; the horns of the North at dawn
panels.style: Massed cavalry shock — 'spears shall be shaken'; shield-wall on foot in extremity (Grimbold at the Fords, Helm's Dike).
panels.cite: UT n.36, Fords of Isen; LotR III, V

panels.org: Thranduil's Woodland host (spears and bows, 'many'), Lorien's Galadhrim border-wards who repelled three assaults, Elrond's household, Cirdan's Havens: hosts of quality without number, fading.
gear: the great bows of Lorien (Galadriel's gift to Legolas)
long white knives of the Galadhrim
the Elvenking's spearmen at the Five Armies
panels.style: Forest denial and archery; river-lines; no field-marches far from home ('the power that dwells there is too great').
panels.arms: Glamdring & Orcrist borne again · Sting · the bow of the Galadhrim
panels.cite: Hobbit XVII; LotR II, App B; UT

panels.org: Mansion-hosts of Erebor and the Iron Hills: Dain's 500 came in mail-hauberks to the knee, bearing mattocks and round shields; beside them Dale's town-levy and the great-bow tradition of Bard.
gear: hauberks of fine ring-mail to the knee
the Black Arrow of Dale; windlance-craft
panels.style: Siege-endurance and the wedge; vendetta-war fought to the last grudge (Azanulbizar), never conquest.
panels.arms: Durin's Axe (lost in Moria) · the Black Arrow
panels.cite: Hobbit XVII; App A III; LotR App B

panels.org: Sauron's tribute-empire in arms: Morgul- and Barad-dur hosts, Olog-hai, the Nazgul as terror-command; Saruman's manufactured 10,000 (Uruk pike-phalanxes, wolf-riders, Dunlending levies, blasting-fire); Harad's mumakil-towers, Easterling axe-hosts, Khand's Variags, Umbar's fifty dromunds.
gear: orc scimitars; Uruk short broad blades and yew bows
the White Hand and the S-rune on shield and helm
the Red Eye branded on the legions
war-towers on the mumakil of Harad
the Black Serpent on scarlet of the Southrons
Grond come again — the wolf-headed ram, a hundred feet
panels.style: Mass, terror and engines: multi-front grand strategy; hosts that break at command-decapitation (Morannon, as Dagorlad before).
panels.arms: Grond the ram · the morgul-knife · scimitars beyond count
panels.cite: LotR III.7-9, V.4-6, V.10; App F

panels.org: A demilitarized gentry with a memory of bows: 12 Shirriffs, the Bounders, the hue-and-cry; one battle in living memory before Bywater. Beside them the un-mustered: Beornings, Woodmen, the Druedain's poisoned darts, the Ents.
gear: hobbit archery — 'keen-eyed and sure at the mark'
the thrown stone; Bywater's banked lanes
Ents & Huorns: fortress-demolition beyond mortal means
panels.style: Ambush, barricade and the land itself; the Ents tore Isengard's gates 'like bread-crust'.
panels.arms: the Bullroarer's club · Sting (on loan to legend)

blades: Fingolfin's — it 'glittered like ice'; seven wounds it gave Morgoth
Foe-hammer, Turgon's blade — borne by Gandalf out of the troll-hoard
Goblin-cleaver, its mate — laid on Thorin's tomb, it warns of foes
the Gondolin long-knife that named itself in Mirkwood
Eol's meteoric steel; it 'spoke' to Turin at the last
Telchar's — it cut a Silmaril from the Iron Crown, and snapped
King's-ire, Thingol's sword, kept by the Kings of Numenor
Icicle, Gil-galad's spear — 'none could withstand it'
Telchar's greatest work; broken under Elendil, reforged the Flame of the West
Theoden's, of the house of Eorl
Eomer's, drawn with 'Death!' at the Pelennor
Beleg's black yew bow, buried with him
of Dor-lomin, Telchar's work — its wearer feared no stroke
the Hammer of the Underworld, Morgoth's; its name given again to the wolf-ram at the Gate
Bard's heirloom 'from the forges of the true king under the Mountain'
